parentQuestion: A patient reports being able to 'feel' someone touching their left hand, but can...

scaffolds.q: The brain processes touch in stages — first detecting that touch occurred, then interpreting where and what was touched.
scaffolds.explain: Sensory processing is hierarchical. Primary sensory cortex detects the raw stimulus (touch occurred). Association cortex interprets it (where, what shape, what texture). You can have one without the other.

scaffolds.q: The somatosensory cortex is located in which lobe?
scaffolds.explain: The primary somatosensory cortex (S1) is located in the postcentral gyrus of the parietal lobe, just behind the central sulcus. It processes touch, pressure, temperature, pain, and proprioception.
